sourceMovie.title: Doubt
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Doubt works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: American Gangster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doubt clicked for you, American Gangster (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Doubt?
faq.answer: Try American Gangster, Rocketman, Gone Baby Gone—each shares DNA with Doubt while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Doubt?
faq.answer: Doubt (2008) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Doubt worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
